message:
fix(refinery): supersede MR on same-branch re-submission with new commits (GH#3032)

When a polecat fixes a failing gate and runs gt done again on the same
branch, the MR idempotency check was treating the re-submission as a
duplicate (same branch name = same MR). This left the Refinery holding
a stale MR pointing at the old failing commits.

Root cause: the @timestamp suffix in polecat branch names is a base-36
millisecond timestamp, NOT a commit SHA. Multiple fix attempts within
the same polecat session share the same branch name, so FindMRForBranch
always found the existing open MR.

Fix: record the branch HEAD SHA as head_sha in the MR description. On
re-submission, compare stored head_sha against current HEAD. If they
differ (new commits), close the stale MR and create a fresh one.
Backward-compatible: old MRs without head_sha continue to behave as
before (idempotent by branch name).

Co-Authored-By: Chris Deal <cdeal@mines.edu>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -462,6 +462,12 @@ type MRFields struct {
 	PreVerified     bool   // Polecat ran full gates after rebasing onto target
 	PreVerifiedAt   string // ISO 8601 timestamp when verification completed
 	PreVerifiedBase string // Target branch SHA at verification time
+
+	// HeadSHA is the git commit SHA at the tip of the source branch when this MR
+	// was submitted. Used to detect re-submissions with new commits on the same
+	// branch name (GH#3032): if the current HEAD differs from HeadSHA, the old MR
+	// is superseded rather than treated as an idempotent duplicate.
+	HeadSHA string
 }
 
 // ParseMRFields extracts structured merge-request fields from an issue's description.
@@ -545,6 +551,9 @@ func ParseMRFields(issue *Issue) *MRFields {
 		case "pre_verified_base", "pre-verified-base", "preverifiedbase":
 			fields.PreVerifiedBase = value
 			hasFields = true
+		case "head_sha", "head-sha", "headsha":
+			fields.HeadSHA = value
+			hasFields = true
 		}
 	}
